mentions.note: Sticky, enterprise-focused B2B AI business with a faster path to profitability than OpenAI, but a rumored $350 billion valuation (30-40x sales) is a monster price to pay.
Jumped from $318 to $335 after-hours on Trump's proposed defense-budget increase; a terrific defense-tech company for caller Mary's inherited IRA.
Down from the mid-60s to the low-40s on inventory-reset and integration worries, but the company just closed an acquisition and could still add another 10 points.
Down almost 100 points from its November highs despite Nvidia's Jensen Huang praising it as the secure foundation of the AI buildout; a rare, aggressive buying opportunity.
Cheap at 16x earnings, but Cramer wants to wait for CEO Jamie Dimon's characteristically cautious earnings-call commentary to knock the stock down before buying.
Liked, but high food inflation across the restaurant sector prevents an emphatic recommendation.
Largest IPO in four years priced healthily and trades modestly above offer; a buy if the stock comes in further.
Stock punished on heavy AI capex spending, now down from $555 to $485 — an uncommon chance to buy a quality AI compounder.
Still losing too much money; only appropriate as a speculative bet for younger investors, otherwise better opportunities exist elsewhere.
Massive spending commitments and cash burn make a near-term IPO both less likely and riskier if it happens; Wall Street may balk once financials are disclosed.
Well off its highs; management does a great job running the business.
Dividend aristocrat down from $180 to $138 on a soft quarter and new CEO; buy in tranches as the yield rises rather than all at once.
Good company priced too richly while unprofitable; the market has soured on money-losing names, so only a small speculative position makes sense.
Great technology and not expensive relative to growth, though it has yet to deliver the explosive move Cramer keeps expecting.
Excellent rocket-launch and satellite-internet business, but an $800 billion IPO valuation with limited financial disclosure makes it too early to call — wait and see.
Struggling near-term on cattle pricing, but still one of the restaurant names Cramer likes.
Missed a quarter without a good explanation and has heavy short interest; pass given persistent food inflation.
Liked despite a recent analyst downgrade; food inflation keeps Cramer from pounding the table.
